data.content:
The build confirms: the file is generated at `obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`, but it's never added to the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn doesn't compile it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `@(Compile)` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The Roslyn compiler doesn't know about the file because it's not included in `@(Compile)`.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group in the target, so the compiler picks it up. I need to add:


Adding the generated file to the Compile item group...

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.content:
**Diagnosis:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it. The generated file wasn't in the `@(Compile)` item group, so Roslyn never saw it.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="..." />` item inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation. Build and run now succeed.
